what_it_does: Defines a React-based Type registry inspector with inline metadata editing, slot and variable editors, scoped and global Vi chat interfaces, and a quick-add modal that drafts or registers Types through global registry and AI helpers.
observations: Uses browser globals such as window.CV_REGISTRY, window.CV_TYPES_VI, window.claude, window.dsaToast, window.TypeThumb, window.TypeLayerBadge, and ViShape.

TypeInspector subscribes to registry changes, resolves the selected Type, shows lineage, thumbnail, metadata, actions, slots, variables, variants, scoped Vi chat, and an advanced-editor footer action.
Built-in Types are shown with disabled inline fields and limited actions, while user Types can be deleted and edited.
Slot editing supports adding, removing, relabeling, toggling multiple/optional, editing accepted layers, previewing matching candidate Types, and requesting slot suggestions from Vi.
Variable editing stores variable rows with key, label, default, and kind fields, applying changes through onChange as inputs change.
Scoped Vi chat turns natural-language prompts into update or create-variant proposals, then waits for an explicit Apply action.
Global Vi rail is present when no context is selected and proposes new Types for the Workshop.
QuickAdder drafts new Types or variants based on parent, shelf, or generic brief context and registers the returned draft.
The file attaches all main components to window through Object.assign.
standouts: Combines visual Type inspection with direct registry mutation controls in one side-panel flow.
Treats slots as compositional drop-zone metadata and previews candidate Types that match each slot.
Embeds a JSON-only AI action protocol for update, create-variant, and answer intents.
Maintains two Vi surfaces: one scoped to the selected Type and one global rail for Workshop-level additions.
conventions: Uses function components and locally scoped helper functions rather than ES module exports.
Aliases React hooks at file top with _ri suffixes.
Uses global services and components through window instead of imports.
Applies registry updates through shallow patch objects passed to R.update.
Uses rs-* and dsa-* class naming for UI structure and shared button styles.
Uses inline event handlers and small nested action functions for UI behavior.
Uses optimistic local state for form inputs, then commits some fields on blur.
concerns: useEffect subscriptions do not guard against missing subscribe beyond optional chaining and rely on the callback return value for cleanup.
The name and description local state reset only on typeId changes, not on all external type mutations for the same id.
Variable edits call onChange without checking canEdit inside setVar and rmVar, relying on disabled controls to prevent use.
AI-provided patches and drafts are applied or registered with minimal validation in this file.
Some nested helper names repeat across components, such as send and applyProposal, which can make symbol references depend on line disambiguation.
Several buttons use text glyphs or abbreviated labels such as ×N, opt, ×, and arrows rather than icon components.
notes: This file is the registry Type inspector and Vi-assisted authoring surface for editing, composing, and adding Types.
questions: What validation does CV_REGISTRY perform when applying update patches or registering AI-generated drafts?
Should built-in Type edits automatically fork, as the hint suggests, or remain disabled as implemented here?
What shape does CV_TYPES_VI.suggestSlots guarantee for suggested slot objects?
How does the advanced editor receive the currently selected Type, since this file only calls onAdvanced?
Should created variants from ViChatScoped be automatically selected after registration?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose matches the implementation: it provides a side-panel inspector, persistent Vi rail, quick adder, visual editing controls, and drag/drop-oriented slot zones, although this file itself shows slot zones and matching previews rather than implementing actual drag/drop handlers.
intention_signals: It appears intended to let users inspect and evolve a Type system conversationally and visually, while keeping AI-generated structural changes reviewable before application.
novelty: The file combines a design-system Type inspector with an embedded assistant that can propose registry patches, create variants, suggest slots, and draft new Workshop Types from natural language.
contribution: Contributes a fused system pattern for file-local Type authoring: inspected registry entities, visual slot and variable controls, lineage and variant navigation, and AI-assisted proposal flows that preserve explicit user application steps.
summary_for_embedding: React file defining a ConceptV registry inspector with Type metadata editing, slot and variable management, variant actions, scoped/global Vi chat, and AI-assisted Type creation.
intention_for_embedding: Built to make Type registry authoring interactive, visual, and conversational while routing changes through registry patch, register, and proposal flows.

symbols.does: Renders the right-side inspector for a selected Type.
symbols.description: It loads the selected Type, resolved Type data, variants, and lineage from CV_REGISTRY, then presents thumbnail, editable metadata, actions, slots, variables, variants, scoped Vi chat, and an advanced-editor action. It also distinguishes built-in Types from editable user Types.

symbols.does: Commits an edited Type name to the registry on blur.
symbols.description: It compares the local name field with the current Type name, trims non-empty input, and calls R.update with a name patch only when the value changed.

symbols.does: Commits an edited Type description to the registry on blur.
symbols.description: It compares the local description field with the current Type description and calls R.update with the new description when changed.

symbols.does: Creates a user-owned copy of the current Type.
symbols.description: It registers a new Type by spreading the current Type, assigning a copy id and name, setting provenance to user, and storing forkedFrom. After registration it shows a toast and navigates to the copied Type.

symbols.does: Creates or delegates creation of a child variant for the current Type.
symbols.description: If onAddVariant is supplied, it delegates variant creation to that callback. Otherwise it registers a new Type extending the current Type, preserving layer, family, icon, and tags while using empty defaults, slots, variables, and a user provenance.

symbols.does: Confirms and removes the current Type from the registry.
symbols.description: It asks for browser confirmation, calls R.remove with the selected typeId, shows a toast, and closes the inspector after deletion.

symbols.does: Renders and manages the slot section for a Type.
symbols.description: It reads the Type slots object, displays count and empty state, lets editable Types add slots or request Vi slot suggestions, and renders each slot through SlotZone with update and removal callbacks.

symbols.does: Applies a patch to one named slot.
symbols.description: It exits when editing is disabled, then merges the existing slots object with a patched entry for the requested slot name and sends the result through onChange.

symbols.does: Removes one named slot from the Type slot map.
symbols.description: It exits when editing is disabled, clones the current slots object, deletes the requested key, and sends the new slots object through onChange.

symbols.does: Adds a default slot to the Type.
symbols.description: It creates a slot name based on the current slot count and initializes it with label New zone, a block-layer acceptance filter, and multiple set to false.

symbols.does: Requests slot suggestions from Vi and merges new suggestions into the Type.
symbols.description: It calls CV_TYPES_VI.suggestSlots for the current Type id, ignores suggested names that already exist, maps suggestion fields into slot metadata, applies the merged slots object, and shows status toasts while tracking a busy state.

symbols.does: Renders controls and previews for a single slot.
symbols.description: It shows an editable slot label, multiple and optional toggles, removal control, accepted layer/family/tag chips, candidate Type thumbnails from R.candidatesForSlot, and an AcceptsQuickEdit control when editing is allowed.

symbols.does: Renders layer toggles for a slot acceptance filter.
symbols.description: It iterates registry layers except template, marks whether each layer is accepted, and toggles that layer in accepts.layers while preserving the rest of the accepts object.

symbols.does: Renders and edits a Type variable list.
symbols.description: It displays a variables count, empty state, add control for editable Types, and a row of key, label, and default inputs for each variable. Changes are emitted as a replacement variables array.

symbols.does: Adds a default variable entry.
symbols.description: It exits when editing is disabled, then appends a text variable with generated key, New variable label, empty default, and kind text.

symbols.does: Patches one variable in the variables array.
symbols.description: It maps over the current variables and merges the patch into the variable at the requested index, then sends the updated array through onChange.

symbols.does: Removes one variable from the variables array.
symbols.description: It filters out the variable at the requested index and sends the reduced variables array through onChange.

symbols.does: Renders a Vi chat interface scoped to the selected Type.
symbols.description: It maintains chat messages, input, busy state, and type-specific suggestions, resets when the Type changes, sends prompts through runViAction, and renders proposals with Apply controls. Applied proposals update the current Type or register a variant.

symbols.does: Sends a scoped Type prompt to Vi and records the response.
symbols.description: It trims user input, appends the user message, calls runViAction with the current Type and prompt, then records applied results, proposals, answers, or error messages in local chat state.

symbols.does: Applies a scoped Vi proposal.
symbols.description: It handles update proposals by calling R.update with the current Type id and patch, and create-variant proposals by registering the provided draft. It then marks the matching chat message as applied and clears its proposal reference.

symbols.does: Builds prompt suggestions for the scoped Vi chat.
symbols.description: It derives up to four suggestion strings from the Type's slots, variables, layer, and family, including slot, template-variable, variant, system-pairing, and widget-kind prompts.

symbols.does: Calls Claude with a Type-scoped JSON action prompt and parses the result.
symbols.description: It builds a prompt containing Type fields and asks for exactly one intent: update, create-variant, or answer. It parses JSON from the model reply and converts it into a local proposal or answer object.

symbols.does: Renders the global Vi rail when no Type context is selected.
symbols.description: It keeps a collapsible assistant panel with starter message, quick ideas, chat messages, input, and proposal application. It returns null whenever a context is present so the scoped inspector chat can take over.

symbols.does: Sends a global Workshop prompt to Vi and records the response.
symbols.description: It trims input, appends the user message, calls runStudioAction, and records either a create-Type proposal or a text response. It also handles busy state and a generic error response.

symbols.does: Applies a global Vi create-Type proposal.
symbols.description: It registers the proposed draft through CV_REGISTRY when the proposal kind is create-type, optionally selects the new Type through onPickType, and marks the proposal message as applied.

symbols.does: Turns a global prompt into a proposed Type draft.
symbols.description: It requires CV_TYPES_VI, calls proposeType with the prompt as a brief, and returns a create-type proposal with a summary. If drafting fails, it returns a text error asking for a more concrete brief.

symbols.does: Renders a modal for adding a new Type or variant from a brief.
symbols.description: It derives prompt context from the adder, station shelf, or parent Type, collects a brief, and offers advanced editor, cancel, and AI-drafting actions. Submitted drafts are registered in the registry and returned through onSaved.

symbols.does: Drafts and registers a Type from the QuickAdder brief.
symbols.description: It chooses proposeType arguments from parent, shelf, or generic context, awaits a draft from CV_TYPES_VI, registers it with CV_REGISTRY, shows a toast, and calls onSaved with the registered Type while tracking busy state.
